House of Black and White (temple underground)
The Lord of Harmony's wooden hall
Twin temples of Semosh and Selloso
Arya Stark (the ugly girl, Cat of the Canals, "no one," Arry, Weasel, Squab, Arya Horseface — all names she recalls)
The old man (insurance underwriter, assassination target)
The shipowner (big, bald, burly, one stiff leg)
The pimply squire from the crossroads inn (referenced)
The guard at Harrenhal (referenced — throat slashed)
A red priest (seen passing at the harbor)
A potbellied innkeep (tries to grab Arya)
A mother with a child (Arya darts past)
A drunk (shoved by the tall guard)
Lysene sailors (staggering from the Happy Port)
A pair of bravos (staggering drunkenly past the docks)
Shrine of the Weeping Lady of Lys
The Lord of Harmony's wooden hall
Temple of Aquan the Red Bull
Twin temples of Semosh and Selloso
House Stark (Arya is identified as "Arya of House Stark")
Faceless Men / servants of the Many-Faced God
The city watch of Braavos (looking for the ugly girl)
Mummers' troupe at the Ship (referenced as abed)
The Many-Faced God / Him of Many Faces / God of Many Faces
The Weeping Lady of Lys (shrine with silver-tear statue)
Three-headed Trios (first head devours the dying, reborn emerge from third)
The Silent God (the Stones of the Silent God)
Aquan the Red Bull (priests slit white calf's throat every thirteenth day, offer bowls of blood to beggars)
Semosh and Selloso (brother gods, twin temples)
The Lord of Harmony (wooden hall with butterfly windows)
R'hllor (implied — a red priest passes in scarlet and crimson robes)
Golden dragon coin (Westerosi — dragon on one face, king on the other)
Acolyte's robe (black on one side, white on the other)
Ebony and weirwood chairs (with carved faces on their backs)
Face-changing magic (deeper than mummer artifice or sorcerer glamors; uses actual skins from the dead; the face becomes "as true and solid as that face you were born with")
Glamors (mentioned as distinct — "sorcerers use glamors, weaving light and shadow and desire to make illusions that trick the eye")
Mummer artifice (mentioned as distinct — changing faces with artifice)
Dead-person memory transfer (Arya experiences the dead girl's memories and pain through the face)
Poisoned coin (mechanism of assassination — causes heart failure when bitten)
Valar morghulis ("All men must die" — used as greeting)
Valar dohaeris ("All men must serve" — used as response/greeting)
Binders (maritime insurance contracts written by the old man)
